subtitle: The westernmost D-Day landing beach, where the U.S. 4th Infantry Division came ashore about 2 km south of its intended sector on 6 June 1944 and lost roughly 200 men taking it — now a quiet swimming and birdwatching beach inside a regional nature park.
spike_statement: The westernmost D-Day beach: the 4th Division landed ~2 km off-target, and Utah cost fewer lives than its own rehearsal at Slapton Sands.
